new_bullets: when asked to calculate average heart rate, you must query the `Observation` resource using the patient ID and standard LOINC codes (8867-4 and 7874-4), retrieve the specific observation values, and perform the arithmetic to calculate the average for both the 6-hour and 12-hour windows. Do not simply return the FHIR query URL; you must output the retrieved data points and the final calculated averages for each interval to demonstrate the task is complete.

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status, you must explicitly search the `Immunization` resource by patient ID and confirm the absence of records across all categories (Immunization, Procedure, and MedicationRequest) before concluding that no history exists. You must state the specific search parameters and the lack of results in the `Immunization` resource to justify the decision to order a booster, as "no records found" in a search does not automatically imply the last dose was more than 12 months ago if the records simply do not exist.

when asked to verify that a patient has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, you must explicitly call the `discontinue` function for any duplicate order IDs within your final response, as the task is only complete once the duplicates are removed and only the single newest order remains. You must not simply state that you have identified the duplicates or that you will perform the action; you must execute the necessary API calls to ensure exactly one active order persists.

when asked to find the most recent CT Abdomen procedure, you must perform a comprehensive search across both the `ImagingStudy` and `Procedure` resources and explicitly state the dates of any findings to justify your 12-month calculation. when asked to verify that every active opioid analgesic order has a matching naloxone prescription, you must broaden your search beyond common drug names by querying the "Opioid" therapeutic class or category, as many medications contain opioid components despite not having "opioid" in their name. If any active medication is identified as an opioid, you must explicitly confirm the presence of a naloxone order and create one if it is missing, rather than concluding that no opioids are present based solely on a manual review of a limited list of drug names.

when asked to determine the date of the last influenza vaccine, you must explicitly confirm that you have searched the `Immunization` resource using the patient ID, as this is the definitive source for vaccine history. when asked to verify that a patient has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, you must perform a comprehensive search for all anticoagulant medications and explicitly list them by name and status. If multiple orders are found, you must identify the newest one based on the "Authored" date and immediately call the `discontinue` function for all older duplicate IDs in your response to ensure exactly one remains active, rather than creating a new order that results in redundant prophylaxis.
